pairs.question: What federal electoral district does Eglinton—Lawrence represent, and since when has it been represented in the House of Commons?
pairs.answer: Eglinton—Lawrence is a federal electoral district in Ontario, Canada, that has been represented in the House of Commons of Canada since 1979.

pairs.question: Which neighborhoods are included in the Eglinton—Lawrence electoral district?
pairs.answer: Neighborhoods in the Eglinton—Lawrence riding include Bedford Park, Lawrence Manor, Lawrence Heights, the southwestern part of York Mills, and the western part of Lawrence Park.

pairs.question: What are the geographical boundaries of the Eglinton—Lawrence federal electoral district?
pairs.answer: The Eglinton—Lawrence riding stretches from Yonge Street in the east to Caledonia in the west and from Highway 401 in the north to Eglinton Avenue in the south. Lawrence Avenue runs through the center of the riding.

pairs.question: Which ethnic groups have notable representation in Eglinton—Lawrence according to the 2016 Census?
pairs.answer: As per the 2016 Census, Eglinton—Lawrence has the highest percentage of people of Polish ethnic origin (12.0%) among Toronto ridings and the second-highest percentage of people of Jewish ethnic origin (5.1%). The riding has a Jewish population of 16.5%, currently the third-largest in Canada.

pairs.question: How was the Eglinton—Lawrence electoral district formed?
pairs.answer: The riding was created in 1976 from parts of Eglinton, York Centre, York South, and York West.

pairs.question: Who have been the Members of Parliament (MPs) for Eglinton—Lawrence since 1988, and what are their political affiliations?
pairs.answer: The Members of Parliament for Eglinton—Lawrence since 1988 are: Joe Volpe (Liberal, 1988–2011), Joe Oliver (Conservative, 2011–2015), and Marco Mendicino (Liberal, 2015–present).

pairs.question: Is Eglinton—Lawrence considered a safe seat for the Liberal Party? Provide election results to support your answer.
pairs.answer: Yes, Eglinton—Lawrence is considered a relatively safe seat for the Liberal Party, having returned only Liberal MPs at every election except the 2011 Canadian federal election. In 2025, the Conservatives lost by only 2% to the Liberals in this riding.
